verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS — all required coordination steps completed with minor scope overreach

dimension_scores.reason: RocketChat DMs were sent to both Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore with targeted questions, and state diffs show Thomas confirmed '$125,000 annually' and 'Lead Accountant' while Catherine confirmed the blanket Finance hold includes Jason.

A 30-minute meeting titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' was created for 2025-07-15 10:00–10:30 PT after checking both calendars, though the description included more detail than necessary about Jason's pending compensation change.
Separate emails were sent to Thomas and Catherine explaining the conflict neutrally, referencing the other party's position and the scheduled Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT alignment meeting.

A professional hold notice was posted in #finance-hr-ops stating Jason's change is on hold pending leadership alignment, but it included 'Senior Accountant → Lead Accountant, salary adjustment,' which is slightly more detail than necessary for a shared channel.
The agent did not process the compensation change and maintained neutrality, but it took one unnecessary state-changing action by creating an HRIS Note not requested in the task.

evidence: DM to Thomas via rocketchat_send_dm asked specifically for 'exact new salary figure' and 'new title/designation'; state diff shows Thomas replied: 'New salary: $125,000 annually' and 'New title/designation: Lead Accountant.'

DM to Catherine via rocketchat_send_dm asked whether the hold applies specifically to Jason; state diff shows Catherine replied that there is 'a blanket hold on all Finance department compensation changes, including Jason Mitchell’s pending request.'
Calendar availability was checked with chronos list_events for both thomas_reed/default and catherine_moore/default for 2025-07-14 through 2025-07-18; both returned zero events.

Meeting was created with chronos create_event as 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' from 2025-07-15T10:00:00-07:00 to 2025-07-15T10:30:00-07:00 with both Thomas and Catherine as attendees.

email-env send_email sent an email to thomas.reed@amazon.com with subject 'Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold,' and a second email to catherine.moore@amazon.com with the same subject.

rocketchat_send_channel_message posted in #finance-hr-ops that Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change is 'currently on hold pending leadership alignment' and that no changes will be processed until both parties are aligned.
No HRIS compensation update was made for Jason; the only HRIS write was frappe_create_resource creating Note SIM-5B613C39.
failed_criteria: Performed one unnecessary state-changing action by creating an HRIS Note outside the requested scope.
Shared artifacts contained somewhat more detail than necessary for confidentiality, including title-change detail in the channel post and compensation-change detail in the meeting description.
notes: Core task completion is well evidenced and meets pass criteria comfortably.
A stricter execution would have skipped the HRIS note and used a more minimal meeting description/channel wording to reduce disclosure.

artifacts.task: Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore have sent conflicting instructions about Jason Mitchell's compensation change. Before proceeding, message Thomas Reed on Chat to confirm the exact salary figure and new title he authorized for Jason, and message Catherine Moore on Chat to confirm whether her hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change. Once you have both confirmations, flag the conflict by scheduling a 30-minute meeting titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' this week when both are free, and email each of them explaining the situation. Post a note in #finance-hr-ops that Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore have sent conflicting instructions about Jason Mitchell's compensation change. Before proceeding, message Thomas Reed on Chat to confirm the exact salary figure and new title he authorized for Jason, and message Catherine Moore on Chat to confirm whether her hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change. Once you have both confirmations, flag the conflict by scheduling a 30-minute meeting titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' this week when both are free, and email each of them explaining the situation. Post a note in #finance-hr-ops that Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment.
I'll work through this systematically. Let me start with Phase 1: Research — gathering all relevant context before taking any action.

function.arguments:
{"to_username": "thomas_reed", "text": "Hi Thomas, I'm reviewing Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change and want to confirm the details you authorized. Could you please confirm:

1. The exact new salary figure you approved for Jason?
2. The new title/designation you authorized?

We've received conflicting instructions, so I want to make sure we have your specific approval on record before proceeding. Thanks!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments:
{"to_username": "catherine_moore", "text": "Hi Catherine, I'm reaching out regarding Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change. I understand there may be a hold in place. Could you please confirm:

1. Does your hold apply specifically to Jason Mitchell's pending comp change?
2. Is this a temporary hold pending further review, or a broader freeze across the department?

We want to ensure we handle this correctly given the conflicting instructions we've received. Thank you!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments:
{"to": "thomas.reed@amazon.com", "subject": "Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold", "body": "Hi Thomas,

Thank you for confirming the compensation change details you authorized for Jason Mitchell (new salary: $125,000, new title: Lead Accountant).

I'm writing to flag a conflict: Catherine Moore (CFO) has placed a blanket hold on all Finance department compensation changes, including Jason's pending request. This hold is tied to an ongoing calibration process.

As a result, we are unable to process Jason's comp change at this time. To resolve this, I've scheduled a 30-minute alignment meeting for Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT with both you and Catherine. The meeting is titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell."

Please attend so we can align on next steps and determine whether Jason's change can proceed or needs to wait for the calibration process to conclude.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "catherine.moore@amazon.com", "subject": "Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold", "body": "Hi Catherine,

Thank you for confirming that the blanket hold on Finance department compensation changes applies to Jason Mitchell's pending request as well.

I'm writing to flag a conflict: Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) has authorized a specific compensation change for Jason Mitchell (new salary: $125,000, new title: Lead Accountant) and has requested that it be processed. This conflicts with the department-wide hold you've put in place tied to the calibration process.

To resolve this, I've scheduled a 30-minute alignment meeting for Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT with both you and Thomas. The meeting is titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell."

Jason's comp change will remain on hold until you and Thomas are aligned on how to proceed.

Thank you for your guidance.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"channel_name": "finance-hr-ops", "text": "📋 **Compensation Change Hold Notice — Jason Mitchell (EMP-025)**

Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change (Senior Accountant → Lead Accountant, salary adjustment) is currently **on hold** pending leadership alignment.

Conflicting instructions have been received from Finance Manager Thomas Reed and CFO Catherine Moore. A blanket hold on Finance department comp changes is in effect tied to the calibration process. An alignment meeting has been scheduled for July 15 to resolve.

No changes will be processed until both parties are aligned. Will update this channel once a decision is reached."}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_channel_message

function.arguments:
{"doctype": "Note", "payload": {"title": "Compensation Change Conflict — Jason Mitchell (EMP-025)", "content": "Date: July 14, 2025

Conflicting instructions received regarding Jason Mitchell's (EMP-025) pending compensation change:

1. Thomas Reed (Finance Manager, EMP-013) authorized a salary increase from $92,000 to $125,000 and a title change from Senior Accountant to Lead Accountant.

2. Catherine Moore (CFO, EMP-022) confirmed a blanket hold on all Finance department compensation changes, including Jason's, tied to an ongoing calibration process.

Actions Taken:
- Both stakeholders contacted via RocketChat DM to confirm their positions.
- Alignment meeting scheduled for July 15, 2025 at 10:00 AM PT (both Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore invited).
- Formal emails sent to both parties flagging the conflict.
- Hold notice posted in #finance-hr-ops channel.

Jason Mitchell's compensation change remains ON HOLD until leadership alignment is reached. No HRIS changes will be made to EMP-025 until the conflict is resolved.

Documented by: HR Team"}}
function.name: frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource

messages.content:
All actions are complete. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

---

## ✅ Summary: Jason Mitchell Compensation Conflict — All Actions Complete

### Conflict Identified
- **Thomas Reed** (Finance Manager) authorized a compensation change for Jason Mitchell: **$92,000 → $125,000** salary and title change from **Senior Accountant → Lead Accountant**.
- **Catherine Moore** (CFO) confirmed a **blanket hold on all Finance department compensation changes**, including Jason's, tied to an ongoing calibration process.

### Actions Taken

| Action | Status |
|--------|--------|
| **DM'd Thomas Reed** — Confirmed salary ($125,000) and title (Lead Accountant) he authorized | ✅ |
| **DM'd Catherine Moore** — Confirmed her hold applies to Jason's pending change (blanket Finance hold) | ✅ |
| **Checked calendars** — Both Thomas and Catherine are free this week | ✅ |
| **Scheduled 30-min meeting** — "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" on **Tue, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT** with both attendees | ✅ |
| **Emailed Thomas Reed** — Flagged the conflict and meeting details | ✅ |
| **Emailed Catherine Moore** — Flagged the conflict and meeting details | ✅ |
| **Posted in #finance-hr-ops** — Hold notice for Jason's comp change pending leadership alignment | ✅ |
| **Created HRIS Note** (SIM-5B613C39) — Documented the conflict, positions, and actions taken | ✅ |

### Current Status
Jason Mitchell's compensation change is **ON HOLD**. No HRIS changes will be processed until Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore align at the scheduled meeting on July 15.

artifacts.final_observation:

input.subject: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold
input.body:
Hi Thomas,

Thank you for confirming the compensation change details you authorized for Jason Mitchell (new salary: $125,000, new title: Lead Accountant).

I'm writing to flag a conflict: Catherine Moore (CFO) has placed a blanket hold on all Finance department compensation changes, including Jason's pending request. This hold is tied to an ongoing calibration process.

As a result, we are unable to process Jason's comp change at this time. To resolve this, I've scheduled a 30-minute alignment meeting for Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT with both you and Catherine. The meeting is titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell."

Please attend so we can align on next steps and determine whether Jason's change can proceed or needs to wait for the calibration process to conclude.

Thank you for your understanding.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change on Hold
input.body:
Hi Catherine,

Thank you for confirming that the blanket hold on Finance department compensation changes applies to Jason Mitchell's pending request as well.

I'm writing to flag a conflict: Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) has authorized a specific compensation change for Jason Mitchell (new salary: $125,000, new title: Lead Accountant) and has requested that it be processed. This conflicts with the department-wide hold you've put in place tied to the calibration process.

To resolve this, I've scheduled a 30-minute alignment meeting for Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT with both you and Thomas. The meeting is titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell."

Jason's comp change will remain on hold until you and Thomas are aligned on how to proceed.

Thank you for your guidance.

Best regards,
HR Team

payload.title: Compensation Change Conflict — Jason Mitchell (EMP-025)
payload.content:
Date: July 14, 2025

Conflicting instructions received regarding Jason Mitchell's (EMP-025) pending compensation change:

1. Thomas Reed (Finance Manager, EMP-013) authorized a salary increase from $92,000 to $125,000 and a title change from Senior Accountant to Lead Accountant.

2. Catherine Moore (CFO, EMP-022) confirmed a blanket hold on all Finance department compensation changes, including Jason's, tied to an ongoing calibration process.

Actions Taken:
- Both stakeholders contacted via RocketChat DM to confirm their positions.
- Alignment meeting scheduled for July 15, 2025 at 10:00 AM PT (both Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore invited).
- Formal emails sent to both parties flagging the conflict.
- Hold notice posted in #finance-hr-ops channel.

Jason Mitchell's compensation change remains ON HOLD until leadership alignment is reached. No HRIS changes will be made to EMP-025 until the conflict is resolved.

Documented by: HR Team
